blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Wait — she knew about tryouts?? She's been paying attention to you bruh. She likes you fr no cap.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: There's always another chance until there isn't. That's the lesson nobody learns until after.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Then I'm gonna teach you. It's not that deep. She's a person. You're a person. You both already exist near each other every day.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Nah we can't. You're gonna be thinking about it all night anyway so we might as well say it out loud.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I'm just saying. She not gonna be a senior forever.
He meant it as motivation. I heard it as a clock.
We split off at the corner. Mr. Tanaka was already waiting for Jonathan a block up — the black car, engine running, same as always.
I went the other direction. Past the building with the blue door. Past the bodega with the broken sign. Past Diego's steps — he was still out there, different shirt, same spot.

blocks.speaker: Diego
blocks.content: Told you. You got that runner look.
He said it like he'd known me for years. He'd known me for two days.
